2305	== Axial Hierarchy Operation Markers
2306	
2307	{axho*} markers appear at the
2308	{axl_detail_site}
2309	as standalone annotations, paralleling the
2310	{axhr*}
2311	regime hierarchy markers.
2312	Each marker uses the prefix-discriminated form and reads ahead a specified number of
2313	{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
2314	from the following text.
2315	
2316	=== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities
2317	
2318	[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
2319	|===
2320	|Marker |Lookahead |Reads
2321	
2322	|{axhob_operation}          |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The operation this detail site describes (no dimensions — dimensions are at {axl_definition_site} only)
2323	|{axhoc_completion}         |0                            |Introduces the signaling contract section (exit codes, status); bare marker, no lookahead
2324	|{axhopt_typed_parameter}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named input parameter whose identity reuses an existing domain type term (zero new terms minted)
2325	|{axhopa_arg_parameter}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named input parameter whose identity reuses a shared CLI argument term (small vocabulary per spec)
2326	|{axhopm_motif_parameter}   |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms); bare marker, no lookahead
2327	|{axhoot_typed_output}      |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named output whose identity reuses an existing domain type term (parallel to typed_parameter pattern)
2328	|{axhoom_motif_output}      |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms); bare marker, no lookahead
2329	|{axhoq_precondition}       |0                            |A required condition before execution; bare marker, no lookahead
2330	|{axhog_guarantee}          |0                            |A promised condition after execution; bare marker, no lookahead
2331	|{axhos_step}               |0                            |A named behavioral step; bare marker, no lookahead
2332	|{axhos_waymark}            |0                            |A branch-targetable step with a local AsciiDoc anchor; bare marker, no lookahead
2333	|===
2334	
2335	[[axhob_operation]]
2336	{axhob_operation}::
2337	Opens the operation hierarchy block at the
2338	{axl_detail_site}.
2339	Lookahead: 1
2340	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2341	— the operation this detail site describes.
2342	The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
2343	{axvo_procedure}
2344	or
2345	{axvo_method}
2346	at the
2347	{axl_definition_site}.
2348	No dimensions on this marker — all dimensions are authoritative at the
2349	{axl_definition_site}
2350	only.
2351	
2352	The surface syntax:
2353	
2354	[source,asciidoc]
2355	----
2356	//axhob_operation
2357	{rbtgo_depot_levy}
2358	
2359	//axhopt_typed_parameter
2360	{rbtgo_depot_levy_name}
2361	----
2362	
2363	[[axhoc_completion]]
2364	{axhoc_completion}::
2365	Marks the signaling contract section in an operation
2366	{axl_detail_site}.
2367	0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; content follows directly.
2368	Documents how the operation communicates success or failure: exit codes for CLI, HTTP status for REST, return values for functions.
2369	Distinct from
2370	{axhog_guarantee}
2371	which describes state promises, and
2372	{axhoot_typed_output}
2373	/{axhoom_motif_output}
2374	which describe produced data.
2375	One per operation.
2376	
2377	[[axhopt_typed_parameter]]
2378	{axhopt_typed_parameter}::
2379	Marks a named input parameter in an operation
2380	{axl_detail_site}
2381	whose lookahead reuses an existing domain type term.
2382	No new term is minted — the attribute reference on the following line names a type already defined elsewhere in the lexicon.
2383	Used when the parameter's identity is fully captured by the type (e.g., a parameter that is simply a CIDR block or a domain name).
2384	Lookahead: 1
2385	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2386	— must resolve to an existing type anchor; zero new terms minted.
2387	Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.
2388	
2389	[[axhopa_arg_parameter]]
2390	{axhopa_arg_parameter}::
2391	Marks a named input parameter in an operation
2392	{axl_detail_site}
2393	whose lookahead reuses a shared CLI argument term.
2394	The attribute reference on the following line names a shared argument from the small CLI argument vocabulary defined per spec; no new term is minted.
2395	Used when the parameter is a well-known CLI argument that appears across multiple operations.
2396	Lookahead: 1
2397	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2398	— must resolve to an existing shared CLI argument anchor; small vocabulary per spec.
2399	Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.
2400	
2401	[[axhopm_motif_parameter]]
2402	{axhopm_motif_parameter}::
2403	Marks an input parameter in an operation
2404	{axl_detail_site}
2405	whose type information is carried on the annotation line itself (motif terms such as dimension modifiers and type classifiers).
2406	0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; type follows inline.
2407	Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.
2408	
2409	[[axhoot_typed_output]]
2410	{axhoot_typed_output}::
2411	Marks a named output in an operation
2412	{axl_detail_site}
2413	whose lookahead reuses an existing domain type term.
2414	Parallel to the
2415	{axhopt_typed_parameter}
2416	pattern: no new term is minted — the attribute reference on the following line names a type already defined elsewhere in the lexicon.
2417	Used when the output's identity is fully captured by the type (e.g., an output that is simply a list of names or a CIDR block).
2418	Lookahead: 1
2419	{mcm_attribute_reference}
2420	— must resolve to an existing type anchor; zero new terms minted.
2421	Individual markers per output; no section wrapper needed.
2422	
2423	[[axhoom_motif_output]]
2424	{axhoom_motif_output}::
2425	Marks an output in an operation
2426	{axl_detail_site}
2427	whose type information is carried on the annotation line itself (motif terms such as dimension modifiers and type classifiers).
2428	0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; type follows inline.
2429	Individual markers per output; no section wrapper needed.
2430	+
2431	May carry
2432	{axd_fact}
2433	to indicate the output is delivered as a named fact file, or
2434	{axd_tally}
2435	to indicate a fact map of dynamically-named files sharing a common infix.
2436	
2437	[[axhoq_precondition]]
2438	{axhoq_precondition}::
2439	Marks a required precondition in an operation
2440	{axl_detail_site}.
2441	No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
2442	Each precondition is a statement about required environmental state before execution.
2443	
2444	[[axhog_guarantee]]
2445	{axhog_guarantee}::
2446	Marks a postcondition guarantee in an operation
2447	{axl_detail_site}.
2448	No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
2449	Each guarantee is a promise about environmental state after successful execution.
2450	
2451	[[axhos_step]]
2452	{axhos_step}::
2453	Marks a named behavioral step in an operation
2454	{axl_detail_site}.
2455	No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
2456	Steps carry their identity through position and prose; no linked term is expected on the following line.
2457	Branching between steps is expressed in prose using cross-references or by directing to a
2458	{axhos_waymark}
2459	anchor.
2460	
2461	[[axhos_waymark]]
2462	{axhos_waymark}::
2463	Variant of
2464	{axhos_step}
2465	for steps that must be branch-targetable from other steps.
2466	No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
2467	Rare; used when prose in another step says "go to step X" and needs a concrete anchor target.
2468	Carries a local AsciiDoc anchor following the `operation_waymarkname` naming convention (e.g., `[[depot_levy_retry]]`).
2469	The anchor enables intra-document cross-references without S0 elevation — the step remains at the detail site level.
2470	
2471	=== Nesting Rules
2472	
2473	Operation hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:
2474	
2475	* {axhob_operation}
2476	opens the operation block; all subsequent markers belong to it until document end or next
2477	{axhob_operation}
2478	* {axhoc_completion},
2479	{axhopt_typed_parameter},
2480	{axhopa_arg_parameter},
2481	{axhopm_motif_parameter},
2482	{axhoot_typed_output},
2483	{axhoom_motif_output},
2484	{axhoq_precondition},
2485	{axhog_guarantee},
2486	and
2487	{axhos_step}
2488	must appear within
2489	{axhob_operation}
2490	scope
2491	* No required ordering among completion, parameter, output, precondition, guarantee, and step markers
2492	* At most one
2493	{axhoc_completion}
2494	per operation
2495	
2496	=== Cross-Document Validation
2497	
2498	* {axhob_operation}
2499	attribute reference must match an operation defined with
2500	{axvo_procedure}
2501	or
2502	{axvo_method}
2503	at the
2504	{axl_definition_site}

== Axial Hierarchy Operation Markers

{axho*} markers appear at the
{axl_detail_site}
as standalone annotations, paralleling the
{axhr*}
regime hierarchy markers.
Each marker uses the prefix-discriminated form and reads ahead a specified number of
{mcm_attribute_reference_s}
from the following text.

=== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities

[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
|===
|Marker |Lookahead |Reads

|{axhob_operation}          |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The operation this detail site describes (no dimensions — dimensions are at {axl_definition_site} only)
|{axhoc_completion}         |0                            |Introduces the signaling contract section (exit codes, status); bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhopt_typed_parameter}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named input parameter whose identity reuses an existing domain type term (zero new terms minted)
|{axhopa_arg_parameter}     |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named input parameter whose identity reuses a shared CLI argument term (small vocabulary per spec)
|{axhopm_motif_parameter}   |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms); bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhoot_typed_output}      |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |A named output whose identity reuses an existing domain type term (parallel to typed_parameter pattern)
|{axhoom_motif_output}      |0                            |Type on annotation line (motif terms); bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhoq_precondition}       |0                            |A required condition before execution; bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhog_guarantee}          |0                            |A promised condition after execution; bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhos_step}               |0                            |A named behavioral step; bare marker, no lookahead
|{axhos_waymark}            |0                            |A branch-targetable step with a local AsciiDoc anchor; bare marker, no lookahead
|===

[[axhob_operation]]
{axhob_operation}::
Opens the operation hierarchy block at the
{axl_detail_site}.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the operation this detail site describes.
The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
{axvo_procedure}
or
{axvo_method}
at the
{axl_definition_site}.
No dimensions on this marker — all dimensions are authoritative at the
{axl_definition_site}
only.

The surface syntax:

[source,asciidoc]
----
//axhob_operation
{rbtgo_depot_levy}

//axhopt_typed_parameter
{rbtgo_depot_levy_name}
----

[[axhoc_completion]]
{axhoc_completion}::
Marks the signaling contract section in an operation
{axl_detail_site}.
0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; content follows directly.
Documents how the operation communicates success or failure: exit codes for CLI, HTTP status for REST, return values for functions.
Distinct from
{axhog_guarantee}
which describes state promises, and
{axhoot_typed_output}
/{axhoom_motif_output}
which describe produced data.
One per operation.

[[axhopt_typed_parameter]]
{axhopt_typed_parameter}::
Marks a named input parameter in an operation
{axl_detail_site}
whose lookahead reuses an existing domain type term.
No new term is minted — the attribute reference on the following line names a type already defined elsewhere in the lexicon.
Used when the parameter's identity is fully captured by the type (e.g., a parameter that is simply a CIDR block or a domain name).
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— must resolve to an existing type anchor; zero new terms minted.
Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.

[[axhopa_arg_parameter]]
{axhopa_arg_parameter}::
Marks a named input parameter in an operation
{axl_detail_site}
whose lookahead reuses a shared CLI argument term.
The attribute reference on the following line names a shared argument from the small CLI argument vocabulary defined per spec; no new term is minted.
Used when the parameter is a well-known CLI argument that appears across multiple operations.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— must resolve to an existing shared CLI argument anchor; small vocabulary per spec.
Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.

[[axhopm_motif_parameter]]
{axhopm_motif_parameter}::
Marks an input parameter in an operation
{axl_detail_site}
whose type information is carried on the annotation line itself (motif terms such as dimension modifiers and type classifiers).
0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; type follows inline.
Individual markers per parameter; no section wrapper needed.

[[axhoot_typed_output]]
{axhoot_typed_output}::
Marks a named output in an operation
{axl_detail_site}
whose lookahead reuses an existing domain type term.
Parallel to the
{axhopt_typed_parameter}
pattern: no new term is minted — the attribute reference on the following line names a type already defined elsewhere in the lexicon.
Used when the output's identity is fully captured by the type (e.g., an output that is simply a list of names or a CIDR block).
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— must resolve to an existing type anchor; zero new terms minted.
Individual markers per output; no section wrapper needed.

[[axhoom_motif_output]]
{axhoom_motif_output}::
Marks an output in an operation
{axl_detail_site}
whose type information is carried on the annotation line itself (motif terms such as dimension modifiers and type classifiers).
0-arity bare marker — no lookahead required; type follows inline.
Individual markers per output; no section wrapper needed.
+
May carry
{axd_fact}
to indicate the output is delivered as a named fact file, or
{axd_tally}
to indicate a fact map of dynamically-named files sharing a common infix.

[[axhoq_precondition]]
{axhoq_precondition}::
Marks a required precondition in an operation
{axl_detail_site}.
No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
Each precondition is a statement about required environmental state before execution.

[[axhog_guarantee]]
{axhog_guarantee}::
Marks a postcondition guarantee in an operation
{axl_detail_site}.
No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
Each guarantee is a promise about environmental state after successful execution.

[[axhos_step]]
{axhos_step}::
Marks a named behavioral step in an operation
{axl_detail_site}.
No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
Steps carry their identity through position and prose; no linked term is expected on the following line.
Branching between steps is expressed in prose using cross-references or by directing to a
{axhos_waymark}
anchor.

[[axhos_waymark]]
{axhos_waymark}::
Variant of
{axhos_step}
for steps that must be branch-targetable from other steps.
No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
Rare; used when prose in another step says "go to step X" and needs a concrete anchor target.
Carries a local AsciiDoc anchor following the `operation_waymarkname` naming convention (e.g., `[[depot_levy_retry]]`).
The anchor enables intra-document cross-references without S0 elevation — the step remains at the detail site level.

=== Nesting Rules

Operation hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:

* {axhob_operation}
opens the operation block; all subsequent markers belong to it until document end or next
{axhob_operation}
* {axhoc_completion},
{axhopt_typed_parameter},
{axhopa_arg_parameter},
{axhopm_motif_parameter},
{axhoot_typed_output},
{axhoom_motif_output},
{axhoq_precondition},
{axhog_guarantee},
and
{axhos_step}
must appear within
{axhob_operation}
scope
* No required ordering among completion, parameter, output, precondition, guarantee, and step markers
* At most one
{axhoc_completion}
per operation

=== Cross-Document Validation

* {axhob_operation}
attribute reference must match an operation defined with
{axvo_procedure}
or
{axvo_method}
at the
{axl_definition_site}

content.content:
1363	== Axial Dimension Terms
1364	
1365	{axd_dimension_s}
1366	are modifiers that appear in
1367	{axl_annotation_s}
1368	to specify characteristics of members and arguments.
1369	Unlike primary
1370	{axl_motif_s},
1371	{axd_dimension_s}
1372	do not stand alone but qualify how a term voices its primary motif.
1373	Applicability of
1374	{axd_dimension_s}
1375	depends on serialization format context.
1376	
1377	[[axd_dimension]]
1378	{axd_dimension}::
1379	A characteristic modifier that qualifies a member or argument in an
1380	{axl_annotation}.
1381	{axd_dimension_s}
1382	appear after the primary
1383	{axl_motif}
1384	in annotation format: `//axl_voices primary dimension ...`
1385	
1386	=== Value Dimensions
1387	
1388	These
1389	{axd_dimension_s}
1390	describe value characteristics and apply across most serialization formats.
1391	They encompass both presence semantics (required, optional, defaulted) and structural semantics (repeated, list).
1392	
1393	[[axd_required]]
1394	{axd_required}::
1395	Member or argument must be present.
1396	Absence triggers validation error or
1397	{axc_fatal}.
1398	
1399	[[axd_optional]]
1400	{axd_optional}::
1401	Member or argument may be omitted.
1402	Absence is valid and handled gracefully.
1403	
1404	[[axd_nullable]]
1405	{axd_nullable}::
1406	Null, nil, or None is a valid value, distinct from absent.
1407	Required for JSON where null is an explicit value type.
1408	Some formats (protobuf) conflate nullable with optional.
1409	
1410	[[axd_none]]
1411	{axd_none}::
1412	Section or field is explicitly declared as having no content.
1413	Distinguishes intentional absence from omission.
1414	Use in section annotations to mark compliance with required sections that have no applicable content.
1415	
1416	[[axd_defaulted]]
1417	{axd_defaulted}::
1418	Has fallback value when absent.
1419	Voicing should specify the default value in prose.
1420	
1421	[[axd_repeated]]
1422	{axd_repeated}::
1423	May have multiple values (array, list, repeated field).
1424	Absence typically means empty collection, not error.
1425	Applies to usage sites (arguments, members) where multiple occurrences are permitted.
1426	
1427	[[axd_list]]
1428	{axd_list}::
1429	Single value containing delimited multiple values.
1430	Distinct from
1431	{axd_repeated}
1432	which indicates multiple separate occurrences.
1433	Applies to type voicings where the type intrinsically represents a delimited collection.
1434	Voicing should specify the delimiter in prose (space-delimited, comma-delimited, etc.).
1435	
1436	=== Format-Specific Dimensions
1437	
1438	These
1439	{axd_dimension_s}
1440	apply only to specific serialization formats.
1441	Using them in forbidden contexts is a type-scheme error.
1442	
1443	[[axd_field_number]]
1444	{axd_field_number}::
1445	Numeric field identifier for protobuf encoding.
1446	Required for protobuf format.
1447	Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.
1448	
1449	[[axd_wire_type]]
1450	{axd_wire_type}::
1451	Protobuf encoding type (varint, fixed32, fixed64, length-delimited).
1452	Required for protobuf format.
1453	Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.
1454	
1455	[[axd_endianness]]
1456	{axd_endianness}::
1457	Byte order for binary encoding (big-endian, little-endian).
1458	Required for binary format.
1459	Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, protobuf.
1460	
1461	[[axd_xml_form]]
1462	{axd_xml_form}::
1463	Whether XML member is attribute or child element.
1464	Required for XML format.
1465	Forbidden for JSON, YAML, protobuf, binary.
1466	
1467	=== Lifecycle Dimensions
1468	
1469	These
1470	{axd_dimension_s}
1471	qualify
1472	{axo_procedure}
1473	voicings to specify execution lifecycle.
1474	Exactly one lifecycle dimension is required for any
1475	{axo_procedure}
1476	voicing.
1477	Lifecycle dimensions are mutually exclusive.
1478	
1479	[[axd_transient]]
1480	{axd_transient}::
1481	{axo_procedure}
1482	executes and completes.
1483	The default lifecycle for most procedures.
1484	{axhoc_completion}
1485	documents exit status or return value.
1486	
1487	[[axd_longrunning]]
1488	{axd_longrunning}::
1489	{axo_procedure}
1490	runs continuously until explicitly stopped.
1491	{axhoc_completion}
1492	documents startup success criteria, not termination.
1493	Requires
1494	{axhos_step} (startup)
1495	and
1496	{axhos_step} (shutdown)
1497	sections.
1498	May include
1499	{axhos_step} (health, signals, recovery)
1500	sections.
1501	
1502	[[axd_periodic]]
1503	{axd_periodic}::
1504	{axo_procedure}
1505	executes on a schedule.
1506	{axhoc_completion}
1507	applies per-run.
1508	Requires
1509	{axhos_step} (schedule)
1510	section.
1511	May include
1512	{axhos_step} (idempotency, overlap)
1513	sections.
1514	
1515	=== Regime Dimensions
1516	
1517	These
1518	{axd_dimension_s}
1519	qualify regime structure annotations.
1520	
1521	[[axd_conditional]]
1522	{axd_conditional}::
1523	Group activation depends on one or more gate conditions.
1524	A group annotated with
1525	{axd_conditional}
1526	requires corresponding hierarchy markers in the subdocument to include at least one
1527	{axhrgc_gate}
1528	specifying the activation criteria.
1529	
1530	[[axrd_singleton]]
1531	{axrd_singleton}::
1532	Regime has exactly one assignment source.
1533	No enumeration or cross-instance operations apply.
1534	
1535	[[axrd_manifold]]
1536	{axrd_manifold}::
1537	Regime has multiple assignment sources (one per instance).
1538	Enumeration and cross-instance operations are meaningful.
1539	
1540	=== Dimension Applicability
1541	
1542	Per-format guidance for
1543	{axd_dimension}
1544	usage:
1545	
1546	[cols="<,^,^,^,^,^",options="header"]
1547	|===
1548	|Dimension |JSON |Protobuf |XML |Binary |YAML
1549	
1550	|{axd_required}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
1551	|{axd_optional}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
1552	|{axd_nullable}     |required  |implicit  |recommend |context   |recommend
1553	|{axd_defaulted}    |optional  |required  |optional  |optional  |optional
1554	|{axd_repeated}     |recommend |required  |recommend |recommend |recommend
1555	|{axd_list}         |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional
1556	|{axd_field_number} |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
1557	|{axd_wire_type}    |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
1558	|{axd_endianness}   |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden
1559	|{axd_xml_form}     |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden
1560	|===
1561	
1562	Legend: required = must specify; recommend = should specify; optional = may specify; implicit = format handles; context = depends on usage; forbidden = not applicable.
1563	
1564	== Axial Format Terms
1565	
1566	{axf_format_s}
1567	specify how configuration data is serialized and carried.
1568	{axrg_assignment_s}
1569	use a particular
1570	{axf_format}
1571	to encode
1572	{axrg_variable}

file.content:
== Axial Dimension Terms

{axd_dimension_s}
are modifiers that appear in
{axl_annotation_s}
to specify characteristics of members and arguments.
Unlike primary
{axl_motif_s},
{axd_dimension_s}
do not stand alone but qualify how a term voices its primary motif.
Applicability of
{axd_dimension_s}
depends on serialization format context.

[[axd_dimension]]
{axd_dimension}::
A characteristic modifier that qualifies a member or argument in an
{axl_annotation}.
{axd_dimension_s}
appear after the primary
{axl_motif}
in annotation format: `//axl_voices primary dimension ...`

=== Value Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
describe value characteristics and apply across most serialization formats.
They encompass both presence semantics (required, optional, defaulted) and structural semantics (repeated, list).

[[axd_required]]
{axd_required}::
Member or argument must be present.
Absence triggers validation error or
{axc_fatal}.

[[axd_optional]]
{axd_optional}::
Member or argument may be omitted.
Absence is valid and handled gracefully.

[[axd_nullable]]
{axd_nullable}::
Null, nil, or None is a valid value, distinct from absent.
Required for JSON where null is an explicit value type.
Some formats (protobuf) conflate nullable with optional.

[[axd_none]]
{axd_none}::
Section or field is explicitly declared as having no content.
Distinguishes intentional absence from omission.
Use in section annotations to mark compliance with required sections that have no applicable content.

[[axd_defaulted]]
{axd_defaulted}::
Has fallback value when absent.
Voicing should specify the default value in prose.

[[axd_repeated]]
{axd_repeated}::
May have multiple values (array, list, repeated field).
Absence typically means empty collection, not error.
Applies to usage sites (arguments, members) where multiple occurrences are permitted.

[[axd_list]]
{axd_list}::
Single value containing delimited multiple values.
Distinct from
{axd_repeated}
which indicates multiple separate occurrences.
Applies to type voicings where the type intrinsically represents a delimited collection.
Voicing should specify the delimiter in prose (space-delimited, comma-delimited, etc.).

=== Format-Specific Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
apply only to specific serialization formats.
Using them in forbidden contexts is a type-scheme error.

[[axd_field_number]]
{axd_field_number}::
Numeric field identifier for protobuf encoding.
Required for protobuf format.
Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.

[[axd_wire_type]]
{axd_wire_type}::
Protobuf encoding type (varint, fixed32, fixed64, length-delimited).
Required for protobuf format.
Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, binary.

[[axd_endianness]]
{axd_endianness}::
Byte order for binary encoding (big-endian, little-endian).
Required for binary format.
Forbidden for JSON, XML, YAML, protobuf.

[[axd_xml_form]]
{axd_xml_form}::
Whether XML member is attribute or child element.
Required for XML format.
Forbidden for JSON, YAML, protobuf, binary.

=== Lifecycle Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
qualify
{axo_procedure}
voicings to specify execution lifecycle.
Exactly one lifecycle dimension is required for any
{axo_procedure}
voicing.
Lifecycle dimensions are mutually exclusive.

[[axd_transient]]
{axd_transient}::
{axo_procedure}
executes and completes.
The default lifecycle for most procedures.
{axhoc_completion}
documents exit status or return value.

[[axd_longrunning]]
{axd_longrunning}::
{axo_procedure}
runs continuously until explicitly stopped.
{axhoc_completion}
documents startup success criteria, not termination.
Requires
{axhos_step} (startup)
and
{axhos_step} (shutdown)
sections.
May include
{axhos_step} (health, signals, recovery)
sections.

[[axd_periodic]]
{axd_periodic}::
{axo_procedure}
executes on a schedule.
{axhoc_completion}
applies per-run.
Requires
{axhos_step} (schedule)
section.
May include
{axhos_step} (idempotency, overlap)
sections.

=== Regime Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
qualify regime structure annotations.

[[axd_conditional]]
{axd_conditional}::
Group activation depends on one or more gate conditions.
A group annotated with
{axd_conditional}
requires corresponding hierarchy markers in the subdocument to include at least one
{axhrgc_gate}
specifying the activation criteria.

[[axrd_singleton]]
{axrd_singleton}::
Regime has exactly one assignment source.
No enumeration or cross-instance operations apply.

[[axrd_manifold]]
{axrd_manifold}::
Regime has multiple assignment sources (one per instance).
Enumeration and cross-instance operations are meaningful.

=== Dimension Applicability

Per-format guidance for
{axd_dimension}
usage:

[cols="<,^,^,^,^,^",options="header"]
|===
|Dimension |JSON |Protobuf |XML |Binary |YAML

|{axd_required}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
|{axd_optional}     |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend |recommend
|{axd_nullable}     |required  |implicit  |recommend |context   |recommend
|{axd_defaulted}    |optional  |required  |optional  |optional  |optional
|{axd_repeated}     |recommend |required  |recommend |recommend |recommend
|{axd_list}         |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional  |optional
|{axd_field_number} |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
|{axd_wire_type}    |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden
|{axd_endianness}   |forbidden |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden
|{axd_xml_form}     |forbidden |forbidden |required  |forbidden |forbidden
|===

Legend: required = must specify; recommend = should specify; optional = may specify; implicit = format handles; context = depends on usage; forbidden = not applicable.

== Axial Format Terms

{axf_format_s}
specify how configuration data is serialized and carried.
{axrg_assignment_s}
use a particular
{axf_format}
to encode
{axrg_variable}

1201	
1202	[[axi_command_interface]]
1203	{axi_command_interface}::

1205	{axl_motif}

1209	[[axi_subcommand]]

1229

1245	
1246	[[axi_hash_parameter]]
1247	{axi_hash_parameter}::

1249	{axl_motif}

1252	
1253	[[axi_request_body]]
1254	{axi_request_body}::

1256	{axl_motif}

1277	and behavior.

1280	{axi_cli_program}::

1284	Invoked from a shell with arguments.

== Axial Interface Terms

[[axi_command_interface]]
{axi_command_interface}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing complete specification of a command's invocation pattern.
Defines syntax and semantics for command usage.

[[axi_subcommand]]
{axi_subcommand}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing nested command within parent command structure.
Inherits context from parent command.

[[axi_command_signature]]
{axi_command_signature}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing complete parameter specification for a command.
Serves as contract for command invocation.

[[axi_endpoint]]
{axi_endpoint}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing URL path accepting HTTP requests.
Defines resource location and accepted operations.

[[axi_api_method]]
{axi_api_method}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing HTTP verb combined with
{axi_endpoint}
specification.
Includes request and response specifications.

[[axi_url_parameter]]
{axi_url_parameter}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing query string key-value pair in URL.
Appears after `?` in URL structure.

[[axi_hash_parameter]]
{axi_hash_parameter}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing fragment identifier for client-side state.
Persists state without server round-trips.

[[axi_request_body]]
{axi_request_body}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing structured input payload for HTTP requests.
Validated against
{axj_structure}
definitions.

[[axi_response_body]]
{axi_response_body}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing structured output payload from HTTP responses.
Conforms to documented
{axj_structure}.

[[axi_cli_interface]]
{axi_cli_interface}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing command-line invocation pattern specification.
Documents
{axa_argument_s}
and behavior.

[[axi_cli_program]]
{axi_cli_program}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Unix-style command-line program.
Invoked from a shell with arguments.
Writes results to stdout, errors to stderr.
Returns exit status: 0 for success, non-zero for failure.
Suitable for scripted, non-interactive use.

[[axi_cli_command_group]]
{axi_cli_command_group}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a namespace within a CLI that contains subcommands.
Does not perform operations itself; serves as organizational container.
May be nested within an
{axi_cli_program}
or another
{axi_cli_command_group}.
Inherits Unix CLI semantics from parent.

[[axi_cli_subcommand]]
{axi_cli_subcommand}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a leaf operation within an
{axi_cli_program}
or
{axi_cli_command_group}.
Performs an actual operation; not a container for other commands.
Inherits Unix CLI semantics from parent: arguments, stdout, stderr, exit status.

[[axi_rest_api]]
{axi_rest_api}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing resource-based web service interface.
Maps operations to HTTP methods and resources.

[[axi_rpc_interface]]
{axi_rpc_interface}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing remote procedure call pattern for service invocation.
Emphasizes operation semantics over HTTP methods.

[[axi_sse_endpoint]]
{axi_sse_endpoint}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing server-sent event stream for push notifications.
Enables real-time updates to connected clients.

[[axi_cc_slash_command]]
{axi_cc_slash_command}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a Claude Code slash command skill artifact.
Implemented as a `.claude/commands/*.md` file containing orchestration logic that the LLM executes as a skill.
Slash commands coordinate multiple
{axi_cli_subcommand}
invocations, gather context, and apply workflow policies.

[[axi_cc_claudemd_verb]]
{axi_cc_claudemd_verb}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing an action verb defined in a
{axi_cc_claudemd_section}
that maps user intent to tool-layer operations.
The verb exists as a concept in the LLM context — either mapping directly to a single
{axi_cli_subcommand}
(direct verb) or orchestrating multiple operations (multistep verb implemented by a
{axi_cc_slash_command}).

[[axi_cc_claudemd_section]]
{axi_cc_claudemd_section}::
A
{axl_motif}
representing a managed section within a project CLAUDE.md file.
Provides persistent LLM context that bridges specification-layer concepts to runtime execution.
Typically contains command references, vocabulary mappings, and operational guidance.

== Axial Dimension Terms

1	= Axial Lexicon Specification
2	:doctype: book
3	
4	// tag::mapping-section[]
5	//
6	// Category declarations:
7	// axc_: Axial Control (orchestration control terms) → mcm_form_deflist
8	// axe_: Axial Environment (execution context patterns) → mcm_form_deflist
9	// axo_: Axial Operation (operation patterns) → mcm_form_deflist, mcm_form_section for sequences
10	// axt_: Axial Type (type system definitions) → mcm_form_deflist
11	// axr_: Axial Record (record/member structure patterns) → mcm_form_section for records, mcm_form_deflist for members
12	// axj_: Axial JSON (JSON-specific concerns) → mcm_form_section for structures, mcm_form_bullet for fields
13	// axa_: Axial Argument (command argument patterns) → mcm_form_deflist, mcm_form_bullet under parent
14	// axi_: Axial Interface (interface patterns) → mcm_form_deflist
15	// axk_: Axial Key Premise (design constraints) → mcm_form_deflist
16	// axl_: Axial Lexicon (motif vocabulary terms) → mcm_form_deflist
17	// axs_: Axial Section (documentation section motifs) → mcm_form_deflist
18	// axd_: Axial Dimension (member/argument characteristic modifiers) → mcm_form_deflist
19	// axf_: Axial Format (serialization/carriage formats) → mcm_form_deflist
20	// axrg_: Axial Regime (configuration system patterns) → mcm_form_deflist
21	// axvr_: Axial Voicing Regime (definition-site regime annotations) → mcm_form_deflist
22	// axvo_: Axial Voicing Operation (definition-site operation annotations) → mcm_form_deflist
23	// axhr*_: Axial Hierarchy Regime (standalone hierarchy markers) → mcm_form_deflist
24	// axho*_: Axial Hierarchy Operation (standalone operation hierarchy markers) → mcm_form_deflist
25	// axpo*_: Axial Product-of-Operation (definition-site voicings for operation outputs) → mcm_form_deflist
26	// axve_: Axial Voicing Entity (definition-site entity annotations) → mcm_form_deflist
27	// axhe*_: Axial Hierarchy Entity (standalone entity hierarchy markers) → mcm_form_deflist
28	// xref_: External specification references
29	//
30	// Extended Type Categories (domain-specific type subcategories):
31	// axtu_: Axial Type Universal (generic infrastructure types) → mcm_form_deflist

33	// axtw_: Axial Type AWS (Amazon Web Services types) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]

35	// axth_: Axial Type GitHub (GitHub types) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]

39	// axig_: Axial Infrastructure Google (GCP infrastructure resources) → mcm_form_deflist
40	// axiw_: Axial Infrastructure AWS (AWS infrastructure resources) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]
41	// axia_: Axial Infrastructure Azure (Azure infrastructure resources) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]
42	// axih_: Axial Infrastructure GitHub (GitHub infrastructure resources) → mcm_form_deflist [reserved]
43	//

45	//   _s: plural form
46	//   _p: possessive form
47	//

input.old_string:
[[axrd_manifold]]
{axrd_manifold}::
Regime has multiple assignment sources (one per instance).
Enumeration and cross-instance operations are meaningful.

=== Dimension Applicability
input.new_string:
[[axrd_manifold]]
{axrd_manifold}::
Regime has multiple assignment sources (one per instance).
Enumeration and cross-instance operations are meaningful.

=== Protocol Dimensions

These
{axd_dimension_s}
qualify
{axi_protocol}
voicings to specify transport, lifecycle, and concurrency presumptions of the bilateral discipline.

[[axd_file_bus]]
{axd_file_bus}::
Transport dimension on an
{axi_protocol}:
participants communicate via a shared filesystem directory acting as the wire.
The caller writes call envelopes as files; the callee observes atomic file appearance via rename(2) (write-temp-then-rename) and writes return envelopes the same way.
No sockets, no in-process shared memory — POSIX file I/O is the entire wire.
+
*Index discipline:* participants coordinate transaction ordering via monotonic indices assigned by the caller and embedded in filenames or envelope bodies.
Indices are session-local and reset on callee restart; the caller detects restart via index-collision or an explicit readiness exchange.
Filename conventions for call and return envelopes are declared in the voicing spec, not in this motif.
+
*Atomicity:* every envelope write uses the rename(2) idiom so receivers never observe partial content.
+
*Wire-format neutrality:*
{axd_file_bus}
is transport-only; envelope contents are described by separate
{axr_record_s}
voicings (e.g.,
{axr_record_json}).

[[axd_disjoint_lifecycle]]
{axd_disjoint_lifecycle}::
Lifecycle dimension on an
{axi_protocol}:
the caller and callee have independent start and stop lifecycles.
Neither actor can assume the other exists when it itself begins;
each call must handle the possibility that the peer is absent, starting, or no longer the same instance that handled the previous call.
+
Implies the caller must observe callee presence before committing to a transaction, and must gracefully degrade or retry when the callee is unavailable.
The specific observation mechanism (readiness probe, file-presence check, timeout) is declared in the protocol's detail site, not in this motif.
+
Contrasts with shared-session protocols where caller and callee share a bounded lifetime (e.g., an HTTP request-response pair scoped to a single process).

=== Dimension Applicability

file.content:
{axl_definition_site}
only
* When a
{axvo_method}
uses
{axd_grouped},
the method's affiliated entity must equal the group's affiliated entity

== Axial Voicing Entity Terms

Three annotation families enable entity specification across
{axl_definition_site_s}
and
{axl_detail_site_s},
paralleling the regime and operation annotation patterns.

Entity specifications describe objects with both structure (fields) and behavior (methods).
Methods within an entity may be *scoped* or *elevated*:

* A *scoped* method is defined entirely within the entity subdocument.
It has no parent-document linked term — its identity exists only within the entity's contract, expressed through position and prose.

* An *elevated* method possesses a linked term at the
{axl_definition_site}
in the parent document, voiced with
{axvo_method}.
Elevated methods earn independent addressability when other spec layers (procedures, dispatch protocols) need to reference them.

A well-constrained entity is its own documentation.
Scoped methods are the default; elevation is the exception for methods that must be independently addressable.

== Axial Voicing Entity Terms
input.new_string:
* When a
{axvo_method}
uses
{axd_grouped},
the method's affiliated entity must equal the group's affiliated entity

== Axial Hierarchy Protocol Markers

{axhp*} markers appear at the
{axl_detail_site}
of an
{axi_protocol}
as standalone annotations, paralleling the
{axho*}
operation hierarchy markers.
A protocol's structural detail — the roster of transactions, and within each transaction the call envelope and return envelope — is expressed through a four-level nesting of these markers.

=== Marker Terms and Lookahead Arities

[cols="<,^,<",options="header"]
|===
|Marker |Lookahead |Reads

|{axhpb_protocol}   |1 {mcm_attribute_reference}  |The protocol this detail site describes (no dimensions — dimensions are at {axl_definition_site} only)
|{axhpt_transaction}|0                            |A named call/return pairing within the protocol; bare marker, identity carried through heading and prose
|{axhpc_call}       |0                            |The caller-side envelope of an enclosing transaction; bare marker, members listed in following prose
|{axhpr_return}     |0                            |The callee-side envelope of an enclosing transaction; bare marker, members listed in following prose
|===

[[axhpb_protocol]]
{axhpb_protocol}::
Opens the protocol hierarchy block at the
{axl_detail_site}.
Lookahead: 1
{mcm_attribute_reference}
— the protocol this detail site describes.
The referenced attribute must correspond to an anchor annotated with
{axi_protocol}
at the
{axl_definition_site}.
No dimensions on this marker — all dimensions (transport, lifecycle, concurrency) are authoritative at the
{axl_definition_site}
only.

The surface syntax:

[source,asciidoc]
----
//axhpb_protocol
{apcswp_wire}

//axhpt_transaction
discerner — main detection transaction.

//axhpc_call
caller payload members:
----

[[axhpt_transaction]]
{axhpt_transaction}::
Marks a named call/return pairing within an
{axi_protocol}
detail site.
No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
A transaction groups exactly one
{axhpc_call}
and one
{axhpr_return}
that together constitute a single caller-initiated exchange.
Transaction identity is carried through section heading and prose; no linked term is expected on the following line.
Multiple
{axhpt_transaction}
blocks within one protocol describe the full set of exchanges the protocol supports.

[[axhpc_call]]
{axhpc_call}::
Marks the caller-side envelope of an enclosing
{axhpt_transaction}.
No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
The prose under this marker enumerates the envelope's members, each referencing a type term defined at the
{axl_definition_site}
(in the parent spec or a shared vocabulary).
Exactly one
{axhpc_call}
per
{axhpt_transaction}.

[[axhpr_return]]
{axhpr_return}::
Marks the callee-side envelope of an enclosing
{axhpt_transaction}.
No lookahead required — bare marker; content follows directly.
The prose under this marker enumerates the envelope's members using the same type-reference pattern as
{axhpc_call}.
Exactly one
{axhpr_return}
per
{axhpt_transaction}.
A transaction where the caller expects no data back still declares
{axhpr_return}
(with prose noting "empty envelope" or equivalent) — the return presence is the signal, and uniform structure keeps the hierarchy complete.

=== Nesting Rules

Protocol hierarchy markers establish implicit scope through document order:

* {axhpb_protocol}
opens the protocol block; all subsequent markers belong to it until document end or next
{axhpb_protocol}
* {axhpt_transaction}
must appear within
{axhpb_protocol}
scope
* {axhpc_call}
and
{axhpr_return}
must appear within
{axhpt_transaction}
scope, each exactly once
* Transactions appear in document order; no required ordering is imposed, but convention places readiness or probe transactions first and main-workload transactions second

=== Cross-Document Validation

* {axhpb_protocol}
attribute reference must match a protocol defined with
{axi_protocol}
at the
{axl_definition_site}
* {axhpb_protocol}
carries no dimensions — all dimensions are authoritative at the
{axl_definition_site}
only
* Types referenced in
{axhpc_call}
and
{axhpr_return}
member prose must resolve to
{axt_type}
anchors in the parent spec or a shared vocabulary

== Axial Voicing Entity Terms

== Overview

This document defines the formal vocabulary for the detection pipeline in Ann's PHI Clipbuddy.
It specifies the entities the algorithm operates on, the resources it consults, the procedures it performs, and the execution sites at which each discerner runs.

The application specification (APCAS) owns the user experience.
The prototype specification (APCPS) owns implementation details, tech stack, data sources, wire-format JSON schemas, and the container image contents.
This document owns the detection pipeline's conceptual structure — the terms that must be precise for the pipeline to be reasoned about correctly.

The pipeline has three named procedures with ten parallel discerners between the first and second.
{apcsgn_normalization}
produces the canonical input.
Ten discerners each run as a pure function of
{apcsgt_normalized_text},
producing their own intimately-named finding type;
seven of them run inside the Rust application and three of them run inside
{apcscc_container}.
{apcsnc_combining}
fuses the ten finding streams into
{apcsnu_unified_finding_s}
covering regions of
{apcsgt_normalized_text}.
{apcsna_anonymization}
produces
{apcsnt_anonymized_text}.

Byte offsets, character arithmetic, and tokenizer mechanics live in code.
This vocabulary talks about regions of
{apcsgt_normalized_text}
— the addressable units of detection — and leaves their concrete representation as an implementation concern.


== Input

{apcsuc_clipboard_content}::
Raw content read from the system clipboard on window focus.

content.content:
412	== Placement and the Container
413	
414	[[apcsgp_placement]]
415	{apcsgp_placement}::
416	The execution site at which a discerner runs.
417	Enumerated: `rust_application` (the discerner runs in-process inside the Tauri-hosted Rust application, emitting findings via direct function calls) and `container` (the discerner runs inside
418	{apcscc_container},
419	emitting findings via a JSON file written to the bind-mounted directory).
420	Every discerner declares its
421	{apcsgp_placement}
422	as part of its definition.
423	{apcsnc_combining}
424	reads both streams into the same
425	{apcsde_evidence}
426	pool;
427	from combining's perspective the
428	{apcsgp_placement}
429	of a finding's originating discerner is a provenance field, not a structural dimension.
430	
431	[[apcscc_container]]
432	{apcscc_container}::
433	A long-running Linux container that hosts the three sophisticated discerners —
434	{apcscs_stanford},
435	{apcscs_spacy},
436	and
437	{apcscs_stanza}
438	— in their native Python runtime.
439	Parameterized by the set of discerner models it bundles (see APCPS for the concrete images, versions, and Dockerfile).
440	Reads inputs from and writes outputs to a single bind-mounted host directory; exposes no network interface (`--network=none`), runs as a non-root user, and drops all Linux capabilities.
441	Lifecycle is managed by tabtargets, not by the Rust application — start, stop, and image build are human-directed actions, distinct from Clipbuddy startup.
442	Communication with the Rust application is file I/O only:
443	Clipbuddy writes
444	{apcsgt_normalized_text}
445	as `{N}-in.txt` into the shared directory;
446	the container reads the highest-indexed
447	`{N}-in.txt`
448	it finds, runs its three discerners, and publishes their consolidated findings atomically as `{N}.json`
449	(write to `{N}.json.tmp`, then `rename(2)` to `{N}.json`).
450	The container emits a running log to `container-log.txt` in the same directory; the start-container tabtarget truncates the log at each container restart.
451	No sockets, no pipes, no HTTP — POSIX file I/O is the entire wire format.
452	See APCPS for the directory path, JSON schema, Dockerfile, and tabtarget set.
453	
454	
455	== Discerners
456	
457	Each discerner is an independent algorithm that consumes
458	{apcsgt_normalized_text}
459	and emits an intimately-named finding type.
460	Ten discerners run in parallel on the same input; no discerner refines or filters another's output.
461	{apcsnc_combining}

== Placement and the Container

[[apcsgp_placement]]
{apcsgp_placement}::
The execution site at which a discerner runs.
Enumerated: `rust_application` (the discerner runs in-process inside the Tauri-hosted Rust application, emitting findings via direct function calls) and `container` (the discerner runs inside
{apcscc_container},
emitting findings via a JSON file written to the bind-mounted directory).
Every discerner declares its
{apcsgp_placement}
as part of its definition.
{apcsnc_combining}
reads both streams into the same
{apcsde_evidence}
pool;
from combining's perspective the
{apcsgp_placement}
of a finding's originating discerner is a provenance field, not a structural dimension.

[[apcscc_container]]
{apcscc_container}::
A long-running Linux container that hosts the three sophisticated discerners —
{apcscs_stanford},
{apcscs_spacy},
and
{apcscs_stanza}
— in their native Python runtime.
Parameterized by the set of discerner models it bundles (see APCPS for the concrete images, versions, and Dockerfile).
Reads inputs from and writes outputs to a single bind-mounted host directory; exposes no network interface (`--network=none`), runs as a non-root user, and drops all Linux capabilities.
Lifecycle is managed by tabtargets, not by the Rust application — start, stop, and image build are human-directed actions, distinct from Clipbuddy startup.
Communication with the Rust application is file I/O only:
Clipbuddy writes
{apcsgt_normalized_text}
as `{N}-in.txt` into the shared directory;
the container reads the highest-indexed
`{N}-in.txt`
it finds, runs its three discerners, and publishes their consolidated findings atomically as `{N}.json`
(write to `{N}.json.tmp`, then `rename(2)` to `{N}.json`).
The container emits a running log to `container-log.txt` in the same directory; the start-container tabtarget truncates the log at each container restart.
No sockets, no pipes, no HTTP — POSIX file I/O is the entire wire format.
See APCPS for the directory path, JSON schema, Dockerfile, and tabtarget set.


== Discerners

Each discerner is an independent algorithm that consumes
{apcsgt_normalized_text}
and emits an intimately-named finding type.
Ten discerners run in parallel on the same input; no discerner refines or filters another's output.
{apcsnc_combining}

{apcsgt_normalized_text}
as `{N}-in.txt` into the shared directory;
the container reads the highest-indexed
`{N}-in.txt`
it finds, runs its three discerners, and publishes their consolidated findings atomically as `{N}.json`
(write to `{N}.json.tmp`, then `rename(2)` to `{N}.json`).
The container emits a running log to `container-log.txt` in the same directory; the start-container tabtarget truncates the log at each container restart.
No sockets, no pipes, no HTTP — POSIX file I/O is the entire wire format.
See APCPS for the directory path, JSON schema, Dockerfile, and tabtarget set.

toolUseResult.newString:
Communication with the Rust application is governed by
{apcswp_wire};
see that section for transaction shapes, member types, and the call/return discipline.
See APCPS for the concrete bind-mount directory path, Dockerfile, and tabtarget set.


== Wire Protocol

The Rust application and
{apcscc_container}
are two
{axo_actor_s}
with independent lifecycles and no shared process memory.
They coordinate through a bilateral discipline — a *protocol* — whose structure is owned by this section.
The protocol names what can be exchanged and under what presumptions;
it does not name which filesystem directory or which image tag realizes it (those live in APCPS).

[[apcswp_wire]]
//axi_protocol axd_file_bus axd_disjoint_lifecycle
{apcswp_wire}::
The call/return discipline between the caller (Rust application) and the callee
({apcscc_container}).
+
*Transport* is
{axd_file_bus}:
a shared host directory is the wire;
the caller writes call envelopes as files and observes return envelopes as atomic file appearances;
the callee symmetrically observes calls and writes returns.
The directory path is a deployment concern named by APCPS, not by this protocol.
+
*Lifecycle presumption* is
{axd_disjoint_lifecycle}:
the caller cannot assume the callee exists at any point;
each transaction must tolerate callee-absent and callee-restart states;
the callee cannot assume caller persistence either — callers come and go.
+
*Concurrency* is single-tenant by construction:
at most one transaction is in flight at any instant.
The caller issues one call, awaits its return, then may issue the next.
No pipelining, no multiplexing.
+
*Direction* is synchronous from the caller's point of view:
a call is meaningful only when paired with its return;
the caller blocks (or times out) waiting for the callee's atomic reply appearance.
+
The protocol supports two named transactions — a readiness probe and the detection exchange — defined as structured children at the
{axl_detail_site}
(see subdocument).
Type terms referenced by transaction members are defined below.

=== Types

[[apcswt_transaction_index]]
//axt_number
{apcswt_transaction_index}::
A monotonically increasing integer assigned by the caller to each transaction within a session.
The index disambiguates concurrent-in-time but not-in-flight transactions at the filesystem level:
the caller writes call envelopes at index N;
the callee writes return envelopes at the same N;
the caller then advances to N+1.
+
Index is session-local — it resets to its starting value whenever the caller starts fresh.
The callee does not persist an expected index across its own restarts;
under
{axd_disjoint_lifecycle}
the callee accepts whatever index the caller presents and echoes it in the return.
Collision detection (a caller observing a return envelope it did not request) is the caller's signal that the callee was restarted mid-session.

[[apcswt_normalized_text]]
{apcswt_normalized_text}::
The wire-facing view of
{apcsgt_normalized_text}:
the exact string the caller wishes the callee to analyze.
Carried verbatim — the protocol imposes no additional normalization beyond what produced
{apcsgt_normalized_text}
upstream.
Size is bounded only by filesystem and runtime limits;
APCPS documents the practical upper bound for the container's current model configuration.

[[apcswt_finding_list]]
//axd_repeated
{apcswt_finding_list}::
The wire-facing view of the callee's consolidated output:
an ordered list of
{apcsgf_finding_s}
produced by the three container-side discerners
({apcscs_stanford},
{apcscs_spacy},
{apcscs_stanza})
running as pure functions of the call's
{apcswt_normalized_text}.
Each element carries its originating discerner as a provenance field so the caller's
{apcsnc_combining}
step can attribute it correctly.
Empty list is valid (all discerners produced no findings);
absence of the field is not distinguished from empty list.

[[apcswt_model_status]]
{apcswt_model_status}::
The callee's readiness self-report, returned in the readiness transaction's return envelope.
Carries per-discerner boolean readiness flags and — when any discerner is not yet ready — a short diagnostic string naming the unloaded model or the phase the callee is in (loading weights, warming JIT, etc.).
The protocol does not enumerate the status vocabulary;
the callee's publication and the caller's interpretation share a version compatibility contract documented in APCPS alongside the container image tag.

=== Protocol Detail

include::APCSWP-wire.adoc[leveloffset=+1]


== Discerners

content.content:
282	## Container Architecture
283	
284	### Purpose
285	
286	Isolate Python-native NLP algorithms from the Rust application process while preserving a strong privacy and security posture. The container's zero-network, capability-dropped, non-root posture means the sophisticated algorithms can be iterated on and scaled up without widening the trust boundary of Clipbuddy itself.
287	
288	### Lifecycle
289	
290	Container lifecycle is **managed externally from Clipbuddy** — via tabtargets, not by the Tauri app. Clipbuddy presumes the container is already running; if it isn't, the bind-mount directory simply fails to receive `{N}.json` files and combining runs with an empty container-side evidence contribution (graceful degradation — Rust-side evidence still drives the anonymized output).
291	
292	Tabtarget set (names in the `apcw-X` family; concrete names pending mint):
293	
294	| Tabtarget | Purpose |
295	|-----------|---------|
296	| Image build | `docker build` the container image from the Dockerfile in the project tree |
297	| Container start | Truncate `container-log.txt`, `docker run` the image with the bind mount and security flags |
298	| Container stop | `docker stop` + `docker rm` |
299	| Container status | Report whether the container is running, which image it's bound to, whether it can see the bind-mount directory |
300	
301	### Bind Mount
302	
303	**Host directory.** `$HOME/apcjd/` — the journal directory, bind-mounted into the container at a container-local path (e.g., `/work/apcjd/`). Single directory, read+write for both sides.
304	
305	**Container files it reads:**
306	- `{N}-in.txt` — normalized text for the input with index `N`
307	
308	**Container files it writes:**
309	- `{N}.json.tmp` — transient, during assembly
310	- `{N}.json` — consolidated findings, published atomically via `rename(2)`
311	- `container-log.txt` — running diagnostic log
312	
313	**Indexing discipline.** The container scans `$HOME/apcjd/` for `{N}-in.txt` files. It processes **only the highest-indexed** input and ignores all others. This implements a drop-old backlog policy: if Ann copies three things quickly, the container processes only the newest; older inputs are skipped. Cleanup of stale inputs and outputs is manual.
314	
315	### Wire Protocol
316	
317	1. Clipbuddy writes `{N}-in.txt` containing `apcsgt_normalized_text` to `$HOME/apcjd/`.
318	2. The container (in a polling or inotify-driven loop; implementation detail) observes the new file, confirms `N` is the highest index present, and runs all three discerners on its contents.
319	3. The container writes `{N}.json.tmp` with the consolidated findings from all three discerners.
320	4. When all algorithms have completed and the file is closed, the container `rename(2)`s `{N}.json.tmp` to `{N}.json`. POSIX guarantees the rename is atomic within the same filesystem — readers either see the old (absent) state or the final file, never a partially written intermediate.
321	5. Clipbuddy (watching via the `notify` crate) observes `{N}.json` existing, reads and deserializes it, and folds its findings into the evidence pool alongside the Rust-side findings.
322	
323	No sockets, no HTTP, no pipes — POSIX file I/O is the entire wire format.
324	
325	### Container Output JSON Schema
326	
327	The `{N}.json` file has this top-level shape:
328	
329	```json
330	{
331	  "index": 10000,
332	  "stanford": {
333	    "findings": [ /* see Stanford finding schema */ ]
334	  },
335	  "spacy": {
336	    "findings": [ /* see spaCy finding schema */ ]
337	  },
338	  "stanza": {
339	    "findings": [ /* see Stanza finding schema */ ]
340	  }
341	}
342	```
343	
344	No schema version field for the prototype — the shape is pinned to the container image version. If the schema evolves, the image tag changes, and any Clipbuddy built against an older schema is simply incompatible with the newer image.
345	
346	Per-discerner finding schemas:
347	
348	**Stanford finding:**
349	```json
350	{
351	  "text": "Margaret J. Thornton",
352	  "start": 45,
353	  "end": 65,
354	  "label": "PATIENT",
355	  "confidence": 0.987
356	}
357	```
358	- `start`/`end` are character offsets into `apcsgt_normalized_text`.
359	- `label` is one of the 8 native labels (non-`O` values only; `O` tokens are not emitted as findings).
360	- `confidence` is the model's softmax confidence for the dominant label over the span.
361	
362	**spaCy finding.** Two variants share a single discerner namespace; each finding is either token-level or entity-level, distinguished by a `kind` field:
363	
364	```json
365	{
366	  "kind": "token",
367	  "text": "may",
368	  "start": 120,
369	  "end": 123,
370	  "pos": "AUX",
371	  "tag": "MD",
372	  "morph": "VerbType=Mod",
373	  "lemma": "may",
374	  "head": 5,
375	  "dep": "aux"
376	}
377	```
378	
379	```json
380	{
381	  "kind": "entity",
382	  "text": "aspirin",
383	  "start": 84,
384	  "end": 91,
385	  "label": "CHEMICAL"
386	}
387	```
388	
389	**Stanza finding.** Same two-variant pattern:
390	
391	```json
392	{
393	  "kind": "token",
394	  "text": "Margaret",
395	  "start": 45,
396	  "end": 53,
397	  "upos": "PROPN",
398	  "xpos": "NNP",
399	  "feats": "Number=Sing",
400	  "lemma": "Margaret",
401	  "head": 2,
402	  "deprel": "nsubj",
403	  "ner": "B-PERSON"
404	}
405	```
406	
407	```json
408	{
409	  "kind": "entity",
410	  "text": "Margaret J. Thornton",
411	  "start": 45,
412	  "end": 65,
413	  "label": "PERSON"
414	}
415	```
416	
417	These schemas are illustrative for the prototype — concrete field ordering, optionality, and type precision will be fixed by the Rust deserializer types in `apcd/src/` when the consumer is written.
418	
419	### Security Posture
420	
421	| Control | Setting | Rationale |
422	|---------|---------|-----------|

file.content:
## Container Architecture

### Purpose

Isolate Python-native NLP algorithms from the Rust application process while preserving a strong privacy and security posture. The container's zero-network, capability-dropped, non-root posture means the sophisticated algorithms can be iterated on and scaled up without widening the trust boundary of Clipbuddy itself.

### Lifecycle

Container lifecycle is **managed externally from Clipbuddy** — via tabtargets, not by the Tauri app. Clipbuddy presumes the container is already running; if it isn't, the bind-mount directory simply fails to receive `{N}.json` files and combining runs with an empty container-side evidence contribution (graceful degradation — Rust-side evidence still drives the anonymized output).

Tabtarget set (names in the `apcw-X` family; concrete names pending mint):

| Tabtarget | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| Image build | `docker build` the container image from the Dockerfile in the project tree |
| Container start | Truncate `container-log.txt`, `docker run` the image with the bind mount and security flags |
| Container stop | `docker stop` + `docker rm` |
| Container status | Report whether the container is running, which image it's bound to, whether it can see the bind-mount directory |

### Bind Mount

**Host directory.** `$HOME/apcjd/` — the journal directory, bind-mounted into the container at a container-local path (e.g., `/work/apcjd/`). Single directory, read+write for both sides.

**Container files it reads:**
- `{N}-in.txt` — normalized text for the input with index `N`

**Container files it writes:**
- `{N}.json.tmp` — transient, during assembly
- `{N}.json` — consolidated findings, published atomically via `rename(2)`
- `container-log.txt` — running diagnostic log

**Indexing discipline.** The container scans `$HOME/apcjd/` for `{N}-in.txt` files. It processes **only the highest-indexed** input and ignores all others. This implements a drop-old backlog policy: if Ann copies three things quickly, the container processes only the newest; older inputs are skipped. Cleanup of stale inputs and outputs is manual.

### Wire Protocol

1. Clipbuddy writes `{N}-in.txt` containing `apcsgt_normalized_text` to `$HOME/apcjd/`.
2. The container (in a polling or inotify-driven loop; implementation detail) observes the new file, confirms `N` is the highest index present, and runs all three discerners on its contents.
3. The container writes `{N}.json.tmp` with the consolidated findings from all three discerners.
4. When all algorithms have completed and the file is closed, the container `rename(2)`s `{N}.json.tmp` to `{N}.json`. POSIX guarantees the rename is atomic within the same filesystem — readers either see the old (absent) state or the final file, never a partially written intermediate.
5. Clipbuddy (watching via the `notify` crate) observes `{N}.json` existing, reads and deserializes it, and folds its findings into the evidence pool alongside the Rust-side findings.

No sockets, no HTTP, no pipes — POSIX file I/O is the entire wire format.

### Container Output JSON Schema

The `{N}.json` file has this top-level shape:

```json
{
  "index": 10000,
  "stanford": {
    "findings": [ /* see Stanford finding schema */ ]
  },
  "spacy": {
    "findings": [ /* see spaCy finding schema */ ]
  },
  "stanza": {
    "findings": [ /* see Stanza finding schema */ ]
  }
}
```

No schema version field for the prototype — the shape is pinned to the container image version. If the schema evolves, the image tag changes, and any Clipbuddy built against an older schema is simply incompatible with the newer image.

Per-discerner finding schemas:

**Stanford finding:**
```json
{
  "text": "Margaret J. Thornton",
  "start": 45,
  "end": 65,
  "label": "PATIENT",
  "confidence": 0.987
}
```
- `start`/`end` are character offsets into `apcsgt_normalized_text`.
- `label` is one of the 8 native labels (non-`O` values only; `O` tokens are not emitted as findings).
- `confidence` is the model's softmax confidence for the dominant label over the span.

**spaCy finding.** Two variants share a single discerner namespace; each finding is either token-level or entity-level, distinguished by a `kind` field:

```json
{
  "kind": "token",
  "text": "may",
  "start": 120,
  "end": 123,
  "pos": "AUX",
  "tag": "MD",
  "morph": "VerbType=Mod",
  "lemma": "may",
  "head": 5,
  "dep": "aux"
}
```

```json
{
  "kind": "entity",
  "text": "aspirin",
  "start": 84,
  "end": 91,
  "label": "CHEMICAL"
}
```

**Stanza finding.** Same two-variant pattern:

```json
{
  "kind": "token",
  "text": "Margaret",
  "start": 45,
  "end": 53,
  "upos": "PROPN",
  "xpos": "NNP",
  "feats": "Number=Sing",
  "lemma": "Margaret",
  "head": 2,
  "deprel": "nsubj",
  "ner": "B-PERSON"
}
```

```json
{
  "kind": "entity",
  "text": "Margaret J. Thornton",
  "start": 45,
  "end": 65,
  "label": "PERSON"
}
```

These schemas are illustrative for the prototype — concrete field ordering, optionality, and type precision will be fixed by the Rust deserializer types in `apcd/src/` when the consumer is written.

### Security Posture

| Control | Setting | Rationale |
|---------|---------|-----------|
| Network | `--network=none` | The container cannot initiate or accept any network traffic — eliminates PHI exfiltration risk |
| Capabilities | `--cap-drop=all` | No Linux capabilities beyond the bare minimum needed to run Python and write to the bind mount |
| Filesystem | Read-only root filesystem (`--read-only`), writable bind-mount only | The container can mutate only the bind-mounted directory |
| User | Non-root (explicit `USER` directive in Dockerfile) | Standard container hygiene; bind-mount file ownership maps to host UID/GID |
| Resource limits | None (prototype scale) | Memory and CPU caps deferred until empirical usage is observed |

### Rationale Summary

- **Python ecosystem unlocked**: scispaCy, Stanza, HuggingFace transformers run natively without ONNX export ceremony or Rust-Python FFI.

input.new_string:
### Wire Protocol Realization

The wire's abstract contract — transactions, envelopes, types, and the single-tenant synchronous discipline — is defined by `apcswp_wire` in APCS0. This subsection documents the prototype's concrete realization of that contract on top of the `$HOME/apcjd/` bind mount.

**File-bus transport.** The `axd_file_bus` transport is realized by the shared bind-mount directory `$HOME/apcjd/` (host path; mapped to a container-local path). POSIX file I/O is the entire wire — no sockets, no HTTP, no pipes.

**Envelope filenames.** The protocol's two transactions realize as follows:

| Transaction | Caller writes | Callee writes |
|-------------|---------------|---------------|
| Detection | `{N}-in.txt` (plain UTF-8 — `apcswt_normalized_text`) | `{N}.json` (per schema below — carries `apcswt_transaction_index` and `apcswt_finding_list`) |
| Readiness probe | `ping-in` (reserved name, non-indexed) | `ping.json` (carries `apcswt_model_status`) |

The readiness probe's filenames are placeholders; the concrete names are a realization decision pending implementation. The detection transaction uses `{N}-in.txt` / `{N}.json` because the index serves double duty as filename disambiguator and as `apcswt_transaction_index`.

**Atomic publish.** The container writes `{N}.json.tmp` during assembly, then `rename(2)`s to `{N}.json` when all three discerners have completed. POSIX guarantees rename atomicity within the same filesystem — Clipbuddy either sees the file absent or sees it complete, never partially written.

**Return observation.** Clipbuddy uses the `notify` crate to watch `$HOME/apcjd/` for return-envelope appearances. On observing `{N}.json`, it reads, deserializes, and folds the findings into the evidence pool alongside the Rust-side findings.

**Single-tenant under adversity.** APCS0's single-tenant premise is respected on the happy path — Clipbuddy awaits the return before issuing the next call. If Ann copies several things in rapid succession faster than the callee can process them, Clipbuddy *may* write multiple `{N}-in.txt` files ahead of the callee. The container tolerates this by processing **only the highest-indexed `{N}-in.txt` it finds** and skipping the rest (drop-old backlog policy). This preserves the single-tenant premise from the callee's perspective — one transaction in flight at a time — while keeping the caller UI responsive. Cleanup of skipped inputs and stale outputs is manual at the prototype scale.

lines: +This is the concrete JSON realization of the detection transaction's return envelope. APCS0 owns the abstract envelope: it carries `apcswt_transaction_index` (echoed) and `apcswt_finding_list` (the three-discerner output). This subsection defines how those types are serialized for the prototype.
The `{N}.json` file has this top-level shape:
+- `index` realizes `apcswt_transaction_index` — echoed from the call's filename `{N}-in.txt`.

+- The `stanford`/`spacy`/`stanza` object is the prototype's shape for `apcswt_finding_list`: a per-discerner grouping rather than a flat list. Each grouping's `findings` array carries that discerner's findings tagged implicitly by the containing key (the schemas below do not repeat the discerner name on each finding — the parent key is the tag).

No schema version field for the prototype — the shape is pinned to the container image version. If the schema evolves, the image tag changes, and any Clipbuddy built against an older schema is simply incompatible with the newer image.

475	//axi_protocol axd_file_bus axd_disjoint_lifecycle
476	{apcswp_wire}::
477	The call/return discipline between the caller (Rust application) and the callee
478	({apcscc_container}).
479	+
480	*Transport* is
481	{axd_file_bus}:
482	a shared host directory is the wire;
483	the caller writes call envelopes as files and observes return envelopes as atomic file appearances;
484	the callee symmetrically observes calls and writes returns.
485	The directory path is a deployment concern named by APCPS, not by this protocol.
486	+
487	*Lifecycle presumption* is
488	{axd_disjoint_lifecycle}:
489	the caller cannot assume the callee exists at any point;
490	each transaction must tolerate callee-absent and callee-restart states;
491	the callee cannot assume caller persistence either — callers come and go.
492	+
493	*Concurrency* is single-tenant by construction:
494	at most one transaction is in flight at any instant.
495	The caller issues one call, awaits its return, then may issue the next.
496	No pipelining, no multiplexing.
497	+
498	*Direction* is synchronous from the caller's point of view:
499	a call is meaningful only when paired with its return;
500	the caller blocks (or times out) waiting for the callee's atomic reply appearance.
501	+
502	The protocol supports two named transactions — a readiness probe and the detection exchange — defined as structured children at the
503	{axl_detail_site}
504	(see subdocument).
505	Type terms referenced by transaction members are defined below.
506	
507	=== Types
508	
509	[[apcswt_transaction_index]]
510	//axt_number
511	{apcswt_transaction_index}::
512	A monotonically increasing integer assigned by the caller to each transaction within a session.
513	The index disambiguates concurrent-in-time but not-in-flight transactions at the filesystem level:
514	the caller writes call envelopes at index N;
515	the callee writes return envelopes at the same N;
516	the caller then advances to N+1.
517	+
518	Index is session-local — it resets to its starting value whenever the caller starts fresh.
519	The callee does not persist an expected index across its own restarts;
520	under
521	{axd_disjoint_lifecycle}
522	the callee accepts whatever index the caller presents and echoes it in the return.
523	Collision detection (a caller observing a return envelope it did not request) is the caller's signal that the callee was restarted mid-session.
524	
525	[[apcswt_normalized_text]]
526	{apcswt_normalized_text}::
527	The wire-facing view of
528	{apcsgt_normalized_text}:
529	the exact string the caller wishes the callee to analyze.
530	Carried verbatim — the protocol imposes no additional normalization beyond what produced
531	{apcsgt_normalized_text}
532	upstream.
533	Size is bounded only by filesystem and runtime limits;
534	APCPS documents the practical upper bound for the container's current model configuration.
535	
536	[[apcswt_finding_list]]
537	//axd_repeated
538	{apcswt_finding_list}::
539	The wire-facing view of the callee's consolidated output:
540	an ordered list of
541	{apcsgf_finding_s}
542	produced by the three container-side discerners
543	({apcscs_stanford},
544	{apcscs_spacy},
545	{apcscs_stanza})
546	running as pure functions of the call's
547	{apcswt_normalized_text}.
548	Each element carries its originating discerner as a provenance field so the caller's
549	{apcsnc_combining}
550	step can attribute it correctly.
551	Empty list is valid (all discerners produced no findings);
552	absence of the field is not distinguished from empty list.
553	
554	[[apcswt_model_status]]
555	{apcswt_model_status}::
556	The callee's readiness self-report, returned in the readiness transaction's return envelope.
557	Carries per-discerner boolean readiness flags and — when any discerner is not yet ready — a short diagnostic string naming the unloaded model or the phase the callee is in (loading weights, warming JIT, etc.).
558	The protocol does not enumerate the status vocabulary;
559	the callee's publication and the caller's interpretation share a version compatibility contract documented in APCPS alongside the container image tag.
560	
561	=== Protocol Detail
562	
563	include::APCSWP-wire.adoc[leveloffset=+1]
564	
565	
566	== Discerners
567	
568	Each discerner is an independent algorithm that consumes
569	{apcsgt_normalized_text}
570	and emits an intimately-named finding type.
571	Ten discerners run in parallel on the same input; no discerner refines or filters another's output.
572	{apcsnc_combining}
573	fuses their finding streams.
574	
575	There is deliberately no generic supertype for discerners and no generic finding type.
576	Each discerner is named individually, parameterized individually, and reasoned about individually;
577	{apcsnc_combining}
578	rules reference discerners and findings by name, not by a shared abstract shape.
579	This is load-bearing: earlier drafts that introduced `algorithm` and `reliability_profile` abstractions collapsed distinct concerns (what a discerner knows, how confident it is, how its output should be displayed) into non-load-bearing abstractions that each had to be re-specialized at every rule site.
580	
581	[[apcsgf_finding]]
582	{apcsgf_finding}::
583	A single detection emitted by any discerner, covering a region of
584	{apcsgt_normalized_text}.
585	Prose noun — there is no generic schema.
586	Each discerner's
587	{apcsgf_finding}
588	subtype has its own intimate schema carrying whatever information that discerner's algorithm produces.
589	
590	[[apcsde_evidence]]
591	{apcsde_evidence}::
592	The pool of every
593	{apcsgf_finding}
594	emitted by every discerner for a single input.
595	{apcsnc_combining}
596	is a function of
597	{apcsde_evidence}.
598	The pool preserves provenance — a finding's originating discerner is never lost — and allows multiple findings to cover the same region without any one being discarded or subordinated before combining.
599	
600	=== Regex Scan
601	
602	[[apcsds_regex]]
603	{apcsds_regex}::
604	The discerner that detects structurally identifiable PHI by regular-expression pattern matching.
605	Parameterized by the
606	{apcsdt_regex_table}.
607	Scans
608	{apcsgt_normalized_text}
609	against every pattern.
610	Each match emits one
611	{apcsdf_regex}
612	with the
613	{apcsgc_phi_category}
614	specified by the pattern entry.
