//axi_protocol axd_file_bus axd_disjoint_lifecycle
{apcswp_wire}::
The call/return discipline between the caller (Rust application) and the callee
({apcscc_container}).
+
*Transport* is
{axd_file_bus}:
a shared host directory is the wire;
the caller writes call envelopes as files and observes return envelopes as atomic file appearances;
the callee symmetrically observes calls and writes returns.
The directory path is a deployment concern named by APCPS, not by this protocol.
+
*Lifecycle presumption* is
{axd_disjoint_lifecycle}:
the caller cannot assume the callee exists at any point;
each transaction must tolerate callee-absent and callee-restart states;
the callee cannot assume caller persistence either — callers come and go.
+
*Concurrency* is single-tenant by construction:
at most one transaction is in flight at any instant.
The caller issues one call, awaits its return, then may issue the next.
No pipelining, no multiplexing.
+
*Direction* is synchronous from the caller's point of view:
a call is meaningful only when paired with its return;
the caller blocks (or times out) waiting for the callee's atomic reply appearance.
+
The protocol supports two named transactions — a readiness probe and the detection exchange — defined as structured children at the
{axl_detail_site}
(see subdocument).
Type terms referenced by transaction members are defined below.

=== Types

[[apcswt_transaction_index]]
//axt_number
{apcswt_transaction_index}::
A monotonically increasing integer assigned by the caller to each transaction within a session.
The index disambiguates concurrent-in-time but not-in-flight transactions at the filesystem level:
the caller writes call envelopes at index N;
the callee writes return envelopes at the same N;
the caller then advances to N+1.
+
Index is session-local — it resets to its starting value whenever the caller starts fresh.
The callee does not persist an expected index across its own restarts;
under
{axd_disjoint_lifecycle}
the callee accepts whatever index the caller presents and echoes it in the return.
Collision detection (a caller observing a return envelope it did not request) is the caller's signal that the callee was restarted mid-session.

[[apcswt_normalized_text]]
{apcswt_normalized_text}::
The wire-facing view of
{apcsgt_normalized_text}:
the exact string the caller wishes the callee to analyze.
Carried verbatim — the protocol imposes no additional normalization beyond what produced
{apcsgt_normalized_text}
upstream.
Size is bounded only by filesystem and runtime limits;
APCPS documents the practical upper bound for the container's current model configuration.

[[apcswt_finding_list]]
//axd_repeated
{apcswt_finding_list}::
The wire-facing view of the callee's consolidated output:
an ordered list of
{apcsgf_finding_s}
produced by the three container-side discerners
({apcscs_stanford},
{apcscs_spacy},
{apcscs_stanza})
running as pure functions of the call's
{apcswt_normalized_text}.
Each element carries its originating discerner as a provenance field so the caller's
{apcsnc_combining}
step can attribute it correctly.
Empty list is valid (all discerners produced no findings);
absence of the field is not distinguished from empty list.

[[apcswt_model_status]]
{apcswt_model_status}::
The callee's readiness self-report, returned in the readiness transaction's return envelope.
Carries per-discerner boolean readiness flags and — when any discerner is not yet ready — a short diagnostic string naming the unloaded model or the phase the callee is in (loading weights, warming JIT, etc.).
The protocol does not enumerate the status vocabulary;
the callee's publication and the caller's interpretation share a version compatibility contract documented in APCPS alongside the container image tag.

=== Protocol Detail

include::APCSWP-wire.adoc[leveloffset=+1]


== Discerners

Each discerner is an independent algorithm that consumes
{apcsgt_normalized_text}
and emits an intimately-named finding type.
Ten discerners run in parallel on the same input; no discerner refines or filters another's output.
{apcsnc_combining}
fuses their finding streams.

There is deliberately no generic supertype for discerners and no generic finding type.
Each discerner is named individually, parameterized individually, and reasoned about individually;
{apcsnc_combining}
rules reference discerners and findings by name, not by a shared abstract shape.
This is load-bearing: earlier drafts that introduced `algorithm` and `reliability_profile` abstractions collapsed distinct concerns (what a discerner knows, how confident it is, how its output should be displayed) into non-load-bearing abstractions that each had to be re-specialized at every rule site.

[[apcsgf_finding]]
{apcsgf_finding}::
A single detection emitted by any discerner, covering a region of
{apcsgt_normalized_text}.
Prose noun — there is no generic schema.
Each discerner's
{apcsgf_finding}
subtype has its own intimate schema carrying whatever information that discerner's algorithm produces.

[[apcsde_evidence]]
{apcsde_evidence}::
The pool of every
{apcsgf_finding}
emitted by every discerner for a single input.
{apcsnc_combining}
is a function of
{apcsde_evidence}.
The pool preserves provenance — a finding's originating discerner is never lost — and allows multiple findings to cover the same region without any one being discarded or subordinated before combining.

=== Regex Scan

[[apcsds_regex]]
{apcsds_regex}::
The discerner that detects structurally identifiable PHI by regular-expression pattern matching.
Parameterized by the
{apcsdt_regex_table}.
Scans
{apcsgt_normalized_text}
against every pattern.
Each match emits one
{apcsdf_regex}
with the
{apcsgc_phi_category}
specified by the pattern entry.

content.text:
Edits complete. Summary:

**AXLA** (`Tools/cmk/vov_veiled/AXLA-Lexicon.adoc`)
- New `axhp*_` category declaration
- 7 new terms with matching attr + anchor: `axi_protocol` (+`_s`), `axd_file_bus`, `axd_disjoint_lifecycle`, `axhpb_protocol`, `axhpt_transaction`, `axhpc_call`, `axhpr_return`
- New "Protocol Dimensions" subsection under Axial Dimension Terms
- New "Axial Hierarchy Protocol Markers" section (marker table + definitions + nesting + cross-doc rules)

**APCS0** (`Tools/apck/APCS0-SpecTop.adoc`)
- D2 `apcsw_` sub-letter legend (p/x/r/m/t) installed
- 5 new attribute references for `apcswp_wire`, `apcswt_transaction_index`, `apcswt_normalized_text`, `apcswt_finding_list`, `apcswt_model_status`
- `apcscc_container` definition slimmed; wire prose moved out, cites `{apcswp_wire}`
- New "Wire Protocol" section with protocol quoin voicing `//axi_protocol axd_file_bus axd_disjoint_lifecycle`, four type-quoin definitions, and `include::APCSWP-wire.adoc`

**New subdoc** (`Tools/apck/APCSWP-wire.adoc`) — `axhpb_protocol` root with two `axhpt_transaction` sections (Readiness Probe, Detection), each with `axhpc_call` / `axhpr_return` pairs. Members reference the type quoins.

**APCPS** (`Tools/apck/APCPS-PrototypeSpecification.md`)
- Scope declaration updated — APCS0 owns wire contract, APCPS owns realization
- "Wire Protocol" → "Wire Protocol Realization" (cites APCS0; keeps filenames/atomicity/notify/drop-old as realization)
- "Container Output JSON Schema" reframed as concrete JSON realization of `apcswt_finding_list`

**Link check:** all 12 new quoins have matching attribute + anchor. Wire subdoc references all resolve.

**Surfaced during editing — worth flagging:** APCS0's single-tenant premise and APCPS's drop-old backlog policy aren't directly contradictory but sit in tension. I reconciled in APCPS prose ("single-tenant from callee's perspective; drop-old is how the callee tolerates caller-side adversity on rapid clipboard events"). If you read my wording and it doesn't match your mental model, flag it.

**Next steps I'd recommend:**

1. You do a spot-review of the new APCS0 wire section + subdoc — the heart of the pace's deliverable
2. Run `cmsa-normalizer` subagent for whitespace / alignment / sort order (matches ₢A9AAc precedent)
3. When you're satisfied, we notch with a pace-affiliated commit, then you decide about wrap

Want me to launch the normalizer now, or would you prefer to review first?

Both transactions are present and correctly specified in `APCSWP-wire.adoc`:

1. **Readiness Probe transaction** (`=== Readiness Probe`) — caller sends `apcswt_transaction_index`; callee returns index + `apcswt_model_status`
2. **Detection transaction** (`=== Detection`) — caller sends index + `apcswt_normalized_text`; callee returns index + `apcswt_finding_list` (which is the consolidated JSON output from the three container-side discerners: Stanford, spaCy, Stanza)

Notching now.

params.intent: Wire protocol spec: introduced axi_protocol motif with axd_file_bus and axd_disjoint_lifecycle dimensions plus four-level axhp*_ hierarchy family (protocol/transaction/call/return) in AXLA. Authored APCS0 Wire Protocol section with apcswp_wire quoin voicing axi_protocol and four type quoins (transaction_index, normalized_text, finding_list, model_status). Created APCSWP-wire.adoc subdoc with readiness-probe and detection transactions. Rewrote APCPS Container Architecture to cite APCS0 for wire contract, keeping only prototype-specific realization (filenames, atomicity, drop-old tolerance).

message.content: wrap now, and thank you

params.summary: Wire protocol spec settled through user-led design conversation. Key moves: (1) recognized AXLA had no bilateral-binding motif — axe_ is single-actor execution context, axi_rpc_interface is one-sided invocation view, neither names a two-actor relationship; minted axi_protocol. (2) Reframed file_bus from environment-motif to dimension-on-protocol, composable with other transport dimensions. (3) Resolved member-monosemy concern by adopting JJS0's type-quoin pattern — types carry semantics, members are slots referencing types. (4) Distinguished dimensions (qualify quoins) from hierarchy markers (mark structural elements that may or may not be quoins); adopted 4-level axhp*_ family (protocol/transaction/call/return) to match axho*_ operation precedent. (5) Committed to one-subdoc-per-protocol convention, mirroring RBS0's sentry_start pattern. AXLA grew by 7 terms: axi_protocol, axd_file_bus, axd_disjoint_lifecycle, axhpb_protocol, axhpt_transaction, axhpc_call, axhpr_return. APCS0 gained D2 apcsw_ legend, Wire Protocol section with apcswp_wire plus 4 type quoins, and subdoc APCSWP-wire.adoc with readiness-probe and detection transactions. APCPS Container Architecture rewritten to cite APCS0 for wire contract; APCPS retains realization concerns (filenames, atomicity via rename(2), drop-old backlog tolerance). Surfaced tension between APCS0's single-tenant premise and APCPS's drop-old policy; reconciled in APCPS prose as caller-adversity tolerance preserving single-tenant-from-callee's-perspective. Zero code changes, per pace scope. Spook-adjacent observation: the prior pace's size-guard menu (ask-or-split) worked well here — no size trip, but the escalating complexity of a design-heavy pace made incremental confirmation (confirm → do both at once → go for it) the right cadence.
